title: The Heavy Bag at the Fruit Market
synopsis: A child proudly helps at the market, even when the bag becomes heavier than expected.
sentences: Iris went to the fruit market with her grandfather on a bright morning.
They stopped first at a stall with apples, pears, and oranges.
Grandfather asked Iris to help choose the best fruit.
She looked carefully and picked the apples without bruises.
Then they added grapes, bananas, and a small melon to the bag.
When Grandfather handed her the bag, Iris smiled bravely.
After a few steps, she realized it was much heavier than she expected.
She changed hands twice before they reached the next stall.
Grandfather noticed and quietly took some of the weight.
Iris felt relieved, but also proud that she had tried.
At home, they washed the fruit and arranged it in a bowl on the table.
Iris thought it looked even better because she had helped choose it.
